children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), for each fiscal year, the sum appropriated pursuant to remaining after applying shall be allotted by the Secretary for use by cooperating State public welfare agencies which have plans developed jointly by the State agency and the Secretary as follows: The Secretary shall first allot $70,000 to each State, and shall then allot to each State an amount which bears the same ratio to the remainder of such sum as the product of (1) the population of the State under the age of twenty-one and (2) the allotment percentage of the State (as determined under this section) bears to the sum of the corresponding products of all the States.section 625 of this titlesection 628(a) of this title

children.heading: Grants to States to enhance collaboration between State child welfare and juvenile justice systems
children.label: 2 Grants to States to enhance collaboration between State child welfare and juvenile justice systems
A
children.text: the Secretary shall reserve from such excess amount such sums as are necessary for making grants under for such fiscal year, not to exceed $10,000,000; andsection 628c of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the remainder to be applied under paragraph (1) for purposes of making allotments to States for such fiscal year shall be determined after the Secretary first allots $70,000 to each State under such paragraph and reserves such sums under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph.

children.heading: Determination of State allotment percentages
children.label: b Determination of State allotment percentages
children.text: The “allotment percentage” for any State shall be 100 percent less the State percentage; and the State percentage shall be the percentage which bears the same ratio to 50 percent as the per capita income of such State bears to the per capita income of the United States; except that (1) the allotment percentage shall in no case be less than 30 percent or more than 70 percent, and (2) the allotment percentage shall be 70 percent in the case of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, and American Samoa.

children.heading: Promulgation of State allotment percentages
children.label: c Promulgation of State allotment percentages
children.text: The allotment percentage for each State shall be promulgated by the Secretary between October 1 and November 30 of each even-numbered year, on the basis of the average per capita income of each State and of the United States for the three most recent calendar years for which satisfactory data are available from the Department of Commerce. Such promulgation shall be conclusive for each of the two fiscal years in the period beginning October 1 next succeeding such promulgation.

children.heading: United States defined
children.label: d United States defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “United States” means the 50 States and the District of Columbia.

children.label: A
children.text: need sums in excess of the amounts allotted to such other States under the preceding provisions of this section, in carrying out their State plans so developed; and

children.label: B
children.text: will be able to so use such excess sums during the fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: the population under 21 years of age;

children.label: B
children.text: the per capita income of each of such other States as compared with the population under 21 years of age; and

children.label: C
children.text: the per capita income of all such other States with respect to which such a determination by the Secretary has been made.

children.heading: Amounts reallotted to a State deemed part of State allotment
children.label: 3 Amounts reallotted to a State deemed part of State allotment
children.text: Any amount so reallotted to a State is deemed part of the allotment of the State under this section.
